category: multi-variant page where the default variant is a small sampler

consistency.notes: All non-null/non-empty ground-truth fields are supported by the page text. The selected/default variant is the 60g Sampler at $9.50 USD, matching listed price and package size. Roaster location/country are supported by 'Roasted by Ritual Coast Coffee in Portland, Oregon, USA.' Availability is supported by 'In stock.' Boolean fields are consistent with the page: single-origin lot (not a blend), filter roast/whole bean and manual brew emphasis (not espresso), no decaf indication, and no co-ferment/infusion statement.

realism.reasons: This is highly plausible as an html-to-text rendering of a real specialty coffee product page. The structure is very typical: breadcrumb, product title, vendor, price range, selected variant, option selector, quantity/add-to-cart, tasting notes, origin details, descriptive copy, related products, variant block, availability, shipping note, and footer. The voice matches specialty coffee norms well—concise, sensory, origin-forward, and slightly marketing-polished without sounding exaggerated. The coffee details are coherent: Huila/Palestina, Gesha, washed process, 1,850 masl, harvest timing, and floral/citrus/stone-fruit notes all fit together naturally. The producer and farm naming also feel realistic. The pricing ladder is believable for a Gesha, especially with a 60g sampler default and a premium 250g price. The variant data block looks like a plausible extracted ecommerce backend snippet, with consistent IDs, titles, SKUs, package weights, and prices. Minor tiny tells only: 'default_for_inference' is a bit artificial as a label, and '1kg Roaster Bag' sold direct to consumers is slightly less common phrasing than 'bulk' or '1kg bag,' but neither is strong enough to reduce plausibility much.

label.coffee.producer_or_farm: María Fernanda López, Finca El Cedro
label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tastes like: jasmine, white peach, honey, bergamot

In the cup we find a clean floral sweetness: jasmine aromatics, white peach, bergamot and a long honeyed finish. It is delicate but not thin, with a silky texture that shines on pour-over and other manual brew methods.
label.coffee.producer_text: A tiny lot from María Fernanda López in the hills above Palestina, Huila. This Gesha was hand-picked at peak ripeness, fermented briefly in cherry, depulped, washed in clean spring water, and dried slowly on raised beds under shade cloth.
